hero.h1: BMW M47 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The BMW M47 is a 2.0-litre turbo-diesel four — reliable overall, but swirl flaps and turbo wear are its known weak spots. Backed by enquiries in 2025 [EM-VERIFIED]. (Note: The M47 does not appear in the top 62 EM_Proprietary_Data per-engine table. Use [ESTIMATED] and relative language — "hundreds of UK enquiries" — until verified data is available.)

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an M47 typically takes 8–12 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [EM-QUOTE].
costGuide.sharedCostNote: Parts cost is identical across BMW and Land Rover applications; labour time varies slightly by engine bay access.

items.question: How much does a BMW M47 engine replacement cost?
items.answer: A reconditioned M47 typically costs £2,500–£4,500 supply only [EM-QUOTE], varying by variant and specification. Fitting adds £800–£1,600 depending on labour rates and whether the vehicle is BMW or Land Rover.

items.question: Is the BMW M47 a reliable engine?
items.answer: The M47 earns 3.5★ out of 5 based on UK specialist workshop consensus and owner reports — durable when maintained, but swirl flap and turbo issues are well-documented at higher mileages.

items.question: What is the most common BMW M47 failure?
items.answer: Swirl flap failure in the intake manifold is the most common issue — flaps can break and enter the cylinders, causing catastrophic damage. Turbocharger wear and oil leaks also occur from 100,000 miles.

items.question: Which BMW models use the M47?
items.answer: The M47 powers the 1 Series (E87), 3 Series (E46/E90), 5 Series (E39/E60), 7 Series (E65), X3 (E83) and X5 (E53) — plus the Land Rover Freelander TD4.

items.question: Is the BMW M47 the same as Land Rover's version?
items.answer: Yes — the M47R fitted to the Freelander TD4 is BMW's M47 engine with Rover-specific tuning. Both share the same 2.0L block, but Land Rover's M47R uses common-rail injection while early BMW M47s did not.
